stem:
A histogram shows the ages of visitors to a holiday camp. The frequencies for each class interval are:

10–12 years: 6,  12–14 years: 15,  14–16 years: 11,  16–18 years: 8

How many visitors are aged 12–14 years?
hint: Find the bar (or row) for the 12–14 years interval and read its height (frequency).
explanation: The frequency for the 12–14 years class interval is 15, so 15 visitors are in that age group.

stem:
A histogram shows the masses of parcels delivered to an office. The frequencies are:

0–2 kg: 8,  2–4 kg: 13,  4–6 kg: 7,  6–8 kg: 2

How many parcels have a mass in the 4–6 kg interval?
hint: Read the frequency for the 4–6 kg bar directly from the table or histogram.
explanation: The frequency for the 4–6 kg class interval is 7, so 7 parcels have a mass in that range.

stem:
A histogram shows the time (minutes) customers spent in a shop. The frequencies are:

0–10: 4,  10–20: 11,  20–30: 18,  30–40: 9,  40–50: 3

How many customers spent 20–30 minutes in the shop?
hint: Locate the 20–30 minute interval and read off its frequency.
explanation: The frequency for the 20–30 minute interval is 18, so 18 customers spent that amount of time in the shop.

stem:
A histogram shows the daily maximum temperatures recorded over 40 days. The frequencies are:

15–20°C: 5,  20–25°C: 12,  25–30°C: 16,  30–35°C: 7

How many days had a maximum temperature in the 25–30°C interval?
hint: Read the frequency for the 25–30°C bar.
explanation: The frequency for the 25–30°C class interval is 16, so 16 days had a maximum temperature in that range.

stem:
A frequency table shows the number of hours students spent on a school project:

0–2 hours: 3,  2–4 hours: 11,  4–6 hours: 8,  6–8 hours: 3

Which class interval is the modal class?
hint: The modal class is the class interval with the highest frequency.
explanation: The frequencies are 3, 11, 8, 3. The highest frequency is 11, which belongs to the 2–4 hours interval. This is the modal class.

stem:
A frequency table shows the lengths of leaves collected from a garden:

3–5 cm: 4,  5–7 cm: 9,  7–9 cm: 14,  9–11 cm: 8,  11–13 cm: 5

Which is the modal class?
hint: Find the class interval with the greatest frequency.
explanation: The frequencies are 4, 9, 14, 8, 5. The highest frequency is 14, in the 7–9 cm interval. This is the modal class.

stem:
A frequency table shows the distances (km) students travel to school:

0–2 km: 5,  2–4 km: 12,  4–6 km: 8,  6–8 km: 5

Calculate the approximate mean distance using class midpoints. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
hint: The midpoints are 1, 3, 5 and 7. Multiply each midpoint by its frequency, add the products, then divide by the total frequency.

stem:
A frequency table shows the test scores of 25 students:

40–50: 2,  50–60: 5,  60–70: 9,  70–80: 6,  80–90: 3

Calculate the approximate mean score using class midpoints. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
hint: The midpoints are 45, 55, 65, 75 and 85. Multiply each midpoint by its frequency, sum the products, then divide by 25.

stem:
A histogram shows daily rainfall (mm) recorded over 40 days. The frequencies are:

0–5 mm: 18,  5–10 mm: 10,  10–15 mm: 5,  15–20 mm: 4,  20–25 mm: 3

Which term best describes the shape of this distribution?
hint: Look at where the bulk of the data sits and which side the tail extends towards. A tail on the right means positively skewed.
explanation: The frequencies decrease from left to right (18, 10, 5, 4, 3), with most data in the lower intervals and a tail extending to the right. This is a positively skewed (right-skewed) distribution.

stem:
A histogram shows the points scored by a basketball team across 45 games. The frequencies are:

40–50: 1,  50–60: 2,  60–70: 6,  70–80: 15,  80–90: 21

Which term best describes the shape of this distribution?
hint: Look at where the peak is and which side the tail extends towards. A tail on the left means negatively skewed.
explanation: The frequencies increase from left to right (1, 2, 6, 15, 21), with most data in the higher intervals and a tail extending to the left. This is a negatively skewed (left-skewed) distribution.

stem:
A histogram shows the wait times (minutes) of customers at a bus stop. The frequencies are:

0–5: 4,  5–10: 10,  10–15: 14,  15–20: 10,  20–25: 4

Which term best describes the shape of this distribution?
hint: Compare the frequencies on each side of the peak. If both sides mirror each other, the distribution is symmetric.
explanation: The frequencies are 4, 10, 14, 10, 4 — they peak in the middle and decrease equally on both sides. This distribution is symmetric.

stem:
Two classes sat the same test. Their score distributions are:

Class A — 40–50: 2,  50–60: 4,  60–70: 10,  70–80: 6,  80–90: 3
Class B — 40–50: 1,  50–60: 2,  60–70: 5,  70–80: 11,  80–90: 6

Which statement is correct?
options: Class A has a higher modal class than Class B
Class B has a higher modal class than Class A
Both classes have the same modal class
Class A has more students than Class B
answer: Class B has a higher modal class than Class A
hint: Find the modal class for each group by identifying the interval with the highest frequency in each.
explanation:
Class A: highest frequency is 10, in the 60–70 interval. Modal class = 60–70.
Class B: highest frequency is 11, in the 70–80 interval. Modal class = 70–80.
Since 70–80 > 60–70, Class B has a higher modal class than Class A.
